summary: A brief 1931 birthday interview with an elderly Thomas Edison where he reflects on aging with vitality, admits he hasn't heard talking pictures, and declines to comment on Einstein's theory because he doesn't understand it.

principles.statement: Reserve judgment on concepts you do not comprehend.
principles.rationale: When asked for his opinion on Einstein's theory, Edison refused to offer one simply because he didn't understand the underlying science.
principles.source_quote: I don't think anything of Einstein theory because I can't understand it

principles.statement: Aging is pleasant as long as you maintain your health and spirit.
principles.rationale: Despite the physical troubles of getting older, maintaining your energy and having nothing seriously wrong with you makes old age feel fine.
principles.source_quote: it feels very fine be 4 years of age if you don't have anything to matter with you well I have a little trouble now and then but that's because I'm getting old but I've got a lot of Ginger yet

signature_quotes.text: I have a little trouble now and then but that's because I'm getting old but I've got a lot of Ginger yet
signature_quotes.context: When asked how it feels to be his age on his birthday.

signature_quotes.text: I don't think anything of Einstein theory because I can't understand it
signature_quotes.context: When asked for his opinion on Albert Einstein's theory.
